edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback tail harvester at 8:1 achieved PF=1.442 (ACCEPT). Advisory directive #10 (MAP_RSI_RR_CURVE) explicitly requests 5:1 and 7:1 data points to find the optimal R:R for this template family. Critical insight: MACD's R:R curve peaked at 7:1 (PF=1.712) not 8:1 (PF=1.442) — a 19% PF improvement from finding the true peak. RSI pullback may similarly peak below 8:1. If RSI peaks at 7:1 like MACD, we gain a significant PF improvement for the RSI portfolio. Additionally, the MACD 1h experiment was a breakthrough (PF=1.460 on 1h vs 1.712 on 4h — 83% of the 4h edge retained with higher trade count). RSI pullback on 1h is the natural next test: more trades = more statistical confidence for the fragile RSI portfolio (currently only 38-156 trades per variant). Regime assumption: trending + ranging profitable (matching RSI 8:1 results). Invalidation: if 5:1 AND 7:1 both underperform 8:1 on RSI, the R:R curve shape is monotonically increasing for RSI (unlike MACD), and 8:1 remains optimal.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with 5:1 R:R tail harvester exits. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 7.5x ATR. This is the lower-bound R:R test. At 5:1, the win rate should be higher than 8:1 (where win rate is ~18-28%). If RSI pullback entry quality produces enough winners at this R:R, the lower TP may actually yield better risk-adjusted returns. MACD at 5:1 (the 'moderate' variant) achieved PF=1.353 — if RSI matches or exceeds this, 5:1 is a viable portfolio diversifier with different risk characteristics (more frequent, smaller wins).
entry_long: EMA_50 slope positive (EMA_50 current > EMA_50 previous bar).
RSI_14 <= 40 (pullback into oversold territory within uptrend).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope negative (EMA_50 current < EMA_50 previous bar).
RSI_14 >= 60 (pullback into overbought territory within downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
ADX regime gate: ADX_14 > 15 (trending or strong ranging)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Tail harvester pattern: low win rate compensated by extreme payoff asymmetry.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend filter + EMA_50 slope confirmation — standard RSI pullback baseline
RSI_14 <= 40 long / >= 60 short — pullback into countertrend territory
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 7.5x TP — 5:1 tail harvester R:R curve data point

variants.name: rsi_pullback_tail_harvester_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with 7:1 R:R tail harvester exits. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 10.5x ATR. This is the critical test — 7:1 is where MACD peaked (PF=1.712 vs 1.442 at 8:1). If RSI pullback also peaks at 7:1, it confirms a universal R:R optimum across template families. If it continues climbing toward 8:1, it reveals template-specific R:R dynamics where entry signal frequency matters. Either outcome is strategically valuable for portfolio construction.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with 5:1 R:R tail harvester exits. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 7.5x ATR. The 1h timeframe test for RSI pullback. MACD 1h succeeded brilliantly (6:1=PF 1.460, 7:1=PF 1.712), proving 1h is viable. RSI pullback at 5:1 on 1h should produce significantly more trades than 4h (where RSI pullback has a fragility problem with only 38-156 trades). More trades = more statistical confidence. If this produces 300+ trades with PF > 1.2, it becomes the highest-confidence RSI variant.
